entities.name: SORGHUM
entities.description: Grain sorghum [Sorghum bicolor (L.) Moench], a major cereal staple crop in the arid and semi-arid regions of Ethiopia

entities.name: YIELD
entities.description: The productivity of grain sorghum, which is influenced by soil, climate, topographic factors, and fertilizer application

entities.name: ETHIOPIA
entities.description: The country where the research was conducted, specifically in the arid and semi-arid regions

entities.name: SOIL
entities.description: Soil factors that contribute to low sorghum yields

entities.name: CLIMATE
entities.description: Climate factors that contribute to low sorghum yields

entities.name: TOPOGRAPHIC FACTORS
entities.description: Topographic factors that contribute to low sorghum yields

entities.name: NITROGEN
entities.description: A primary nutrient used in fertilizer application to increase sorghum yield

entities.name: PHOSPHORUS
entities.description: A primary nutrient used in fertilizer application to increase sorghum yield

entities.name: POTASSIUM
entities.description: A nutrient used in fertilizer application to investigate yield response

entities.name: SULPHUR
entities.description: A nutrient used in fertilizer application to investigate yield response

entities.name: ZINC
entities.description: A nutrient used in fertilizer application to investigate yield response

entities.name: LANDSCAPE POSITION
entities.description: The spatial location of farmers' fields (upslope, mid-slope, and foot slope) affecting fertilizer response and yield

entities.name: ON-FARM EXPERIMENTS
entities.description: The experimental approach used to test fertilizer combinations across different landscape positions

entities.name: HAYK
entities.description: A sorghum-growing location in the north-eastern Amhara region of Ethiopia

entities.name: SIRINKA
entities.description: A sorghum-growing location in the north-eastern Amhara region of Ethiopia

entities.name: AMHARA REGION
entities.description: The north-eastern region of Ethiopia where the study was conducted

entities.name: FERTILIZER APPLICATION
entities.description: The application of nutrients (NP, K, S, Zn) to sorghum crops to evaluate yield response

entities.name: SPATIAL VARIATION
entities.description: The variation in yield response observed along different landscape positions

entities.name: LANDSCAPE-BASED FERTILIZER MANAGEMENT
entities.description: A management approach that considers landscape environment to address yield potential variability

relationships.description: Sorghum is the crop for which yield is measured
Sorghum yield is attributed to soil factors
Sorghum yield is attributed to climate factors
Sorghum yield is attributed to topographic factors
Fertilizer application strongly affects sorghum yield
Nitrogen is a component of the fertilizer application
Phosphorus is a component of the fertilizer application
Potassium is a component of the fertilizer application
Sulphur is a component of the fertilizer application
Zinc is a component of the fertilizer application
Landscape position affects sorghum yield variability
Landscape position explains the fertilizer response
On-farm experiments were conducted on sorghum crops
Hayk is located in the Amhara region
Sirinka is located in the Amhara region
Amhara region is located in Ethiopia
Sorghum yield response is strongly linked to spatial variation along landscape positions
Landscape-based fertilizer management is used to respond to yield potential variability
